summary: 2026-07-17 11:23:39 PDT — The concurrent critics, fail-closed join, dedicated schema/artifact, and labelled reconciliation match the spec, and the focused reviewer suite passes. One portability defect means the required atomic promotion is not guaranteed.

findings.title: Temporary files may be promoted non-atomically
findings.claim: The diff creates temporary outputs with bare `mktemp`, which normally places them in the system temporary directory, then moves them into the repository. If those locations are on different filesystems, `mv` becomes a copy-and-delete operation rather than an atomic rename. A reader can therefore observe a partial stable artifact, violating AC1's explicit requirement that validated output be atomically promoted.
findings.suggestion: Create unique temporary files inside `reviews/` so each source and destination are guaranteed to share a filesystem, then rename them to the stable sibling paths after validation. Add a trap to clean up unpromoted temporary files.
